Defender.description: Evaluates a Microsoft Defender security control from collected endpoint evidence.
Defender.risk: A weakened Defender control can reduce malware prevention, detection, or response capability.
Defender.impact: Malicious code may execute or persist with less chance of prevention or detection.
Defender.recommendation: Configure Microsoft Defender according to the approved endpoint security baseline.
Defender.framework_context: Endpoint malware protection and security monitoring.

Firewall.description: Evaluates Windows Firewall configuration from collected profile and logging evidence.
Firewall.risk: Weak host firewall policy can expose services and reduce visibility into blocked traffic.
Firewall.impact: Unauthorized network access or undetected connection attempts may become more likely.
Firewall.recommendation: Enable all applicable firewall profiles and apply the organization-approved inbound and logging policy.
Firewall.framework_context: Host firewall and network boundary protection.

Device Security.description: Evaluates hardware-backed and virtualization-based Windows security evidence.
Device Security.risk: Missing platform protections can weaken boot integrity and credential isolation.
Device Security.impact: Attackers may gain stronger persistence or access to protected credentials.
Device Security.recommendation: Enable supported hardware-backed protections through the approved endpoint baseline.
Device Security.framework_context: Secure Boot, TPM, virtualization-based security, and credential protection.

Protocols.description: Evaluates legacy protocol exposure and Windows network authentication hardening.
Protocols.risk: Legacy or weak protocols can enable credential interception, relay, and downgrade attacks.
Protocols.impact: Credentials or network sessions may be compromised by an adjacent attacker.
Protocols.recommendation: Disable legacy protocols and require the organization-approved secure protocol settings.
Protocols.framework_context: Secure network protocols and authentication hardening.

Remote Access.description: Evaluates Windows remote administration controls and discovered remote access products.
Remote Access.risk: Weak or unapproved remote access paths can provide direct administrative entry to an endpoint.
Remote Access.impact: An attacker may obtain remote control, credentials, or persistent access.
Remote Access.recommendation: Harden required remote services and remove or approve remote access software through policy.
Remote Access.framework_context: Remote administration, encrypted management, and approved software governance.
Remote Access.policy_caveat: Whether a remote access product is approved is organization-specific.

Accounts.description: Evaluates local account, administrator membership, password, and lockout evidence.
Accounts.risk: Weak local account controls can enable password attacks and excessive privilege.
Accounts.impact: An attacker may obtain or retain local administrative access.
Accounts.recommendation: Apply the approved account policy and review privileged local memberships.
Accounts.framework_context: Account lifecycle, authentication policy, and least privilege.
Accounts.policy_caveat: Account thresholds must be aligned with organization policy and operational requirements.

PowerShell.description: Evaluates PowerShell version and security logging configuration.
PowerShell.risk: Legacy PowerShell or missing logging can increase execution and detection risk.
PowerShell.impact: Malicious scripts may execute with reduced forensic visibility.
PowerShell.recommendation: Disable PowerShell 2.0 and configure the approved PowerShell logging policy.
PowerShell.framework_context: Script execution hardening and audit telemetry.
PowerShell.policy_caveat: Execution Policy is recorded as evidence but is not treated as a security boundary.

UAC.description: Evaluates User Account Control policy and remote token filtering evidence.
UAC.risk: Weak UAC settings can reduce the barrier between standard and administrative execution.
UAC.impact: Privilege escalation or silent administrative execution may become easier.
UAC.recommendation: Apply the organization-approved UAC baseline and retain secure desktop prompting.
UAC.framework_context: Privilege elevation and administrative token protection.

Audit.description: Evaluates advanced Windows audit policy coverage for security-relevant events.
Audit.risk: Missing audit events reduce detection, investigation, and accountability.
Audit.impact: Security incidents may remain unnoticed or lack sufficient forensic evidence.
Audit.recommendation: Configure advanced audit policy according to the approved monitoring baseline.
Audit.framework_context: Security event logging and audit policy coverage.
Audit.policy_caveat: Required audit subcategories and retention depend on monitoring and legal requirements.

Updates.description: Evaluates Windows Update freshness, service state, restart state, and release policy evidence.
Updates.risk: Delayed or disabled updates can leave known vulnerabilities unpatched.
Updates.impact: The endpoint may remain exposed to publicly known exploits and reliability defects.
Updates.recommendation: Restore update services and meet the organization-approved scan, install, and restart thresholds.
Updates.framework_context: Patch management and supported Windows release governance.
Updates.policy_caveat: Update age and reboot thresholds are organization-specific.

DEF-003.title: Defender signatures are outdated
DEF-003.policy_caveat: Maximum signature age is defined by the selected policy profile.

DEF-008.title: Tamper protection is unavailable or disabled

FW-005.title: Default inbound firewall action is not block

DEV-002.title: TPM is unavailable or not ready

DEV-004.title: Credential Guard is not running
DEV-004.policy_caveat: Credential Guard applicability depends on hardware, edition, and organization policy.

DEV-005.title: Memory Integrity is disabled
DEV-005.policy_caveat: Memory Integrity applicability depends on hardware and application compatibility.

PROTO-005.title: Insecure SMB guest logons are enabled

PROTO-006.title: LAN Manager authentication level is weak

REMOTE-001.title: RDP is enabled without Network Level Authentication

REMOTE-006.title: Unapproved remote access software is installed

PS-002.title: PowerShell Script Block Logging is disabled

UAC-003.title: UAC secure desktop prompting is disabled

UAC-004.title: Remote local-account token filtering is weakened

AUD-001.title: Advanced audit policy coverage is insufficient

AUD-005.title: Audit policy changes are not audited

UPD-003.title: Windows Update scan is too old

UPD-004.title: Windows Update installation is too old

UPD-006.title: Device is outside the configured Windows release target
